front: Philosopher: originated the term 'cosmopolitan' (citizen of the world) rather than identify with any city-state
Philosopher: conflicting accounts attribute this Cynic's death to eating raw octopus or fighting a dog over octopus
Philosopher: said to have refuted Zeno of Elea's paradoxes against motion by simply getting up and walking
Philosopher: 3rd-century biographer whose Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers is a major surviving source for Greek philosophy, addressed to an 'enthusiastic Platonist'
Philosopher: Book X of this biographer's work preserves Epicurus's surviving letters to Herodotus, Menoeceus, and Pythocles
Philosopher: pre-Socratic from Apollonia who believed air was the fundamental substance but added it was intelligent; Theophrastus called him the last of the physiologoi
Philosopher: fictional professor of 'Things in General' at Weissnichtwo University in Carlyle's Sartor Resartus, who passes through the 'centre of indifference' to the 'Everlasting Yea'
